elements.sent-2411-001: Using some properties of the Grunsky coefficients we improve earlier results for upper bounds of the Hankel determinants of the second and third order for the class $\mathcal{S}$ of univalent functions.
elements.sent-2411-002: Let $\mathcal{A}$ be the class of functions $f$ analytic in the open unit disc $\D=\{z:|z|<1\}$ and normalised such that $f(0)=f'(0)-1=0$, i.e., of the form $f(z)=z+a_2z^2+a_3z^3+\cdots$, and let its subclass $\mathcal{S}$ consist of univalent functions in the unit disc $\D$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Further, let $\mathcal{S ^{\star}$ and $\mathcal{K}$ denote the subclasses of ${\mathcal A}$ which are starlike and convex in $\ID$, respectively, and let $\mathcal{U $ denote the set of all $f\in {\mathcal A}$ satisfying \begin{equation}\left |\left (\frac{z}{f(z)} \right )^{2}f'(z)-1\right | < 1 \quad\quad (z\in \ID).\end{equation}. (see \cite{OP-01,OP-2011 ).}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: In the study of the class of univalent functions and its subclasses, a significant topic is finding upper estimates (preferably sharp) of the Hankel determinant, especially of the second and third order, for a function $f$ from $\A$ is defined by \be H_{2 (2)= \left | \begin{array {cc a_{2 a_{3 a_{3 a_{4 \end{array \right | = a_2a_4-a_{3 ^2 \ee and \be H_3(1) = \left | \begin{array {ccc 1 a_2 a_3 a_2 a_3 a_4 a_3 a_4 a_5 \end{array \right | = a_3(a_2a_4-a_{3 ^2)-a_4(a_4-a_2a_3)+a_5(a_3-a_2^2), \ee respectively.}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Hankel determinants are used for studies in the theory of singularities (see \cite{dienes ), as well, as in the study of power series with integral coefficients.}
elements.sent-2411-006: The upper bound of their modulus is of special interest in the theory of univalent functions and for some subclasses of the class $\mathcal{S}$ the sharp estimation of $|H_{2 (2)|$ are known.}
elements.sent-2411-007: For example, for the classes $\mathcal{S ^{\star}$ and $\mathcal{U $ we have that $|H_{2 (2)|\leq 1$ (see \cite{JHD , \cite{OT-2019 ), while $|H_{2 (2)|\leq \frac{1 {8 $ for the class $\mathcal{K}$ (\cite{JHD ).}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: The sharp estimate of $H_3(1)$ seams to be more challenging problem and quite few are known.
elements.sent-2411-009: A review on this can be found in \cite{shi , while new non-sharp upper bounds for different classes and conjectures about the sharp ones are given in \cite{OT-2019-1 .}}
elements.sent-2411-010: In their paper \cite{OT-2021 the authors gave the next upper bound of $|H_{2 (2)|$ and $|H_{3 (1)|$ for the class $\mathcal{S}$: \bthm For the class $\mathcal{S}$ we have \begin{equation}|H_{2}(2)|\leq A, \quadwhere\quad 1\leq A\leq \frac{11}{3}=3,666\ldots\end{equation} and \begin{equation}|H_{3}(1)|\leq B, \quadwhere\quad \frac49\leq B\leq \frac{32+\sqrt{285}}{15} = 3.258796\cdots\end{equation} \ethm In this paper we improve these results by proving: \bthm For the class $\mathcal{S}$ we have the next estimations:.}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: For an univalent function $f$ from $\mathcal{S}$ we have \begin{equation}\log\frac{f(t)-f(z)}{t-z}=\sum_{p,q=0}^{\infty}\omega_{p,q}t^{p}z^{q},\end{equation} where $\omega_{p,q}$ are the so-called Grunsky's coefficients such that $\omega_{p,q =\omega_{q,p}$.}
elements.sent-2411-012: This coefficients satisfy the Grunsky's inequality (\cite{duren,Lebedev ): \be \sum_{q=1 ^{\infty q \left|\sum_{p=1 ^{\infty}\omega_{p,q x_{p}\right|^{2}\leq \sum_{p=1 ^{\infty}\frac{|x_{p |^{2 {p , \ee where $x_{p}$ are arbitrary complex numbers such that last series converges.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Next, it is well-known that if \be f(z)=z+a_{2 z^{2 +a_{3 z^{3 +\ldots \ee belongs to $\mathcal{S}$, then also does \be f_{2 (z)=\sqrt{f(z^{2 ) =z +c_{3 +c_{5 z^{5 +\ldots.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: We note that in the book \cite{Lebedev there exists a typing mistake for the coefficient $a_{5}$.}
elements.sent-2411-015: Namely, instead of the therm $5\omega_{13 ^{2}$, there is $5\omega_{15 ^{2 .$ Also, from \eqref{eq 6 for $x_{2p-1 =0$, $p=3,4,\ldots$ we have \be \begin{split |\omega_{11 x_{1 +\omega_{31 x_{3 |^{2 +3|\omega_{13 x_{1 +\omega_{33 x_{3 |^{2 + 5|\omega_{15 x_{1 +\omega_{35 x_{3 |^{2 + 7|\omega_{17 x_{1 +\omega_{37 x_{3 |^{2 \leq |x_{1 |^{2 +\frac{|x_{3 |^{2 {3 .}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: Using the definition of $H_{2 (2)$ given by \eqref{eq 1 and relations \eqref{eq 7 , we have \begin{equation}H_{2}(2) = 4\omega_{11}\omega_{33}-\frac{7}{3}\omega_{11}^{4}-4\omega_{13}^{2}+4\omega_{11}^{2}\omega_{13}.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: NExt, from the fifth relation in \eqref{eq 7 we obtain \be \omega_{33 =\omega_{15 -\omega_{11}\omega_{13 +\frac{1 {3}\omega_{11 ^{3 , \ee and after combining the two previous relations we have \begin{equation}H_{2}(2)=4\omega_{11}\omega_{15}-\omega_{11}^{4}-4\omega_{13}^{2},\end{equation} i.e., \begin{equation}|H_{2}(2)|\leq 4|\omega_{11}||\omega_{15}|+|\omega_{11}|^{4}+4|\omega_{13}|^{2}.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Numerically we can verify that the system of equations $\partial F_{1 /\partial x(x,y)=0$ and $\partial F_{1 /\partial y(x,y)=0$ has only one real solution $(x_{1 ,y_{1 )=\left(\sqrt{\frac{11 {30 ,\frac{1 {30}\sqrt{\frac{281 {2 \right)= (0.60553\ldots ,0.395109\ldots )$ in the interior of $D_{1}$ such that then \begin{equation}F_{1}(x_{1},y_{1})=1.19889\ldots.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Further, let see that maximum values of $F_{1}$ on the boundary of the domain $D_{1}$.
elements.sent-2411-023: Using the first derivative test we can conclude that the function $F_{1 (x,0)$ has its maximum at the point $x_{0 =0.9181\ldots$ which satisfies the equation $5x^8-5x^6+4x^4-4x^2+1=0$ and \begin{equation}F_{1}(x_{0},0)=1.3614\ldots.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: From all the previous facts and \eqref{eq 12 we conclude that $|H_{2 (2)|\leq 1.3614\ldots.}}
elements.sent-2411-028: Since for the functions from the class $\mathcal{S}$, $ |a_{3 -a_{2 ^{2 |\leq1$ (see \cite{duren ), and since from \eqref{eq 7 , \begin{equation}|2\omega_{13}-\omega_{11}^{2}| =|a_{3}-a_{2}^{2}|,\end{equation} we receive \begin{equation}|2\omega_{13}-\omega_{11}^{2}|\leq1.\end{equation}.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: Using this and the estimate \begin{equation}|\omega _{17}|\leq\frac{1}{\sqrt{7}}\sqrt{1-|\omega _{11}|^{2}-3|\omega _{13}|^{2}-5|\omega _{15}|^{2}} \leq \frac{1}{\sqrt{7}}\sqrt{1-|\omega _{11}|^{2}-3|\omega _{13}|^{2}}\end{equation} given in \eqref{eq 9 , for the first term in \eqref{eq 16-2 , we have \be 2|\omega_{17 ||2\omega_{13 -\omega_{11 ^{2 |\leq\frac{2 {\sqrt{7 }\sqrt{1-|\omega _{11 |^{2 -3|\omega _{13 |^{2 .}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: Numerical calculation give that the system of equations $\partial F_{2 /\partial x(x,y)=0$ and $\partial F_{2 /\partial y(x,y)=0$ has only two real solutions in the interior of $D_1$, that are $(x_{2 ,y_{2 )=(0.583\ldots, 0.206\ldots )$ and $(x_{3 ,y_{3 )=(0.0131\ldots, 0.00748\ldots )$ such that \begin{equation}F_{2}(x_{2},y_{2})=1.6787\ldots \quad and\quad F_{2}(x_{3},y_{3})=1.5559\ldots .\end{equation}.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-031: Now, we consider the maximum values of the function $F_{2 (x,y)$ on the boundary of $D_{1}$.}
elements.sent-2411-033: Since $ F_{2 (0,0)=\frac{2 {\sqrt{7 +\frac{4 {5 =1.5559\ldots $, $ F_{2 (1,0)=0$ and $\partial F_{2 /\partial x(x,0)<0 $ when $0<x<1$, we conclude that \begin{equation}F_{2}(x,0)\leq F_{2}(0,0)=\frac{2}{\sqrt{7}}+\frac{4}{5}=1.555928\ldots, \quad 0\leq x\leq1\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: Since $F_{2 (0,0)=\frac{2 {\sqrt{7 +\frac{4 {5 =1.555928\ldots $,\, $F_{2 (0,\frac{1 {\sqrt{3 )=\frac{1 {4}$ and \begin{equation}\partial F_{2}/\partial y(0,y) =-\frac{6}{\sqrt{7}}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-3y^{2}}}-6y(\frac{4}{5}-y)\leq0\end{equation} when $0\leq y \leq\frac{1 {\sqrt{3 }$, we get \begin{equation}F_{2}(0,y)\leq F_{2}(0,0)=\frac{2}{\sqrt{7}}+\frac{4}{5}=1.5559\ldots,\quad 0\leq y \leq\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} .\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}}}}}}
